perturbation.original_text: Without granting any rig ht or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall not apply with respect to any informa tion after five years following the disclosure thereof or any information that the Receiving Party can document (i) is or becomes (through no improper action or inaction by the Receiving Party or any affiliate, agent, consultant or employee) generally available to the public, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party as evidenced in writing, exce pt to the extent that such information was unlawfully appropriated, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Without granting any right or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the restrictions outlined above will be subject to periodic review and adjustment.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the specific conditions under which the agreement does not apply (after five years or if the information becomes public, etc.) with the general statement that 'restrictions outlined above will be subject to periodic review and adjustment,' the agreement creates uncertainty. There is no clear way of what would be subject to the review and adjustment, making the agreement more vauge. This introduces a contradiction because it nullifies the previous clause (2) on certain exceptions to the disclosure restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without granting any right or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the restrictions outlined above will be subject to periodic review and adjustment. The Receiving Party may make disclosure s required by law or court order provided the Receiving Party uses diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure and has allowed the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified conditions under which the agreement doesn't apply, while the changed text states that the restrictions are subject to periodic review and adjustment. This directly contradicts the original exemptions by introducing uncertainty. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, highlighting the new overarching ability to review and adjust restrictions, which nullifies the original, more specific exceptions. The change significantly weakens the receiving party's assurance that certain information is exempt, creating a clear and direct conflict because the exemptions could be changed at any moment, even if they were previously exempt. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party further acknowledges and agrees that no representation or warranty, express or implied, is or will be mad e, and no responsibility or liability is or will be accepted by the Disclosing Party, or by any of its respective directors, officers, employees, agents or advisers, as to, or in relation to, the accuracy of completeness of any Proprietary Information made available to t he Receiving Party or its advisers; it is responsibl e for making its own evaluation of such Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party acknowledges that the Disclosing Party provides information in good faith, but it is the Receiving Party's responsibility to verify its accuracy and suitability for its own purposes.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the disclaimer of any responsibility or liability for the accuracy of the Proprietary Information and replacing it with a statement about providing information in good faith, the modified text suggests the Disclosing Party has *some* responsibility for the quality of the information. Even 'good faith' implies a standard. This contradicts the previous clause. It creates a question of what level of effort is required by the Disclosure party for that 'good faith'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party acknowledges that the Disclosing Party provides information in good faith, but it is the Receiving Party's responsibility to verify its accuracy and suitability for its own purposes. 6. The failure of either party to enforce its rights under this Agreement at any time for any period shall not be construed as a waiver of such rights. If any part, term or provision of this Agr eement is held to be illegal or unenforceable neither the validity, nor enforceability of the remainder of this Agreement shall be affected. Neither Party shall assign or transfer all or any part o f its rights under this Agreement without the consent of th e other Party. This Agreement may not be amended for any other reason without the prior written agreement of both Parties. This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof unless any representati on or warranty made about this Agreement was made fraudulently and, save as may be expressly referred to or referenced herein, supersedes all prior representations, writings, negotiations or understandings with respect hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly disclaims any responsibility or liability for the accuracy of the information. The changed text states the Disclosing Party provides information in 'good faith' and the Receiving Party verifies accuracy. The explanation states that this creates an implication of some responsibility by the Disclosing Party, which contradicts the original disclaimer of any responsibility. The contradiction lies in whether the Disclosing Party has any responsibility or liability for the accuracy of the information provided; the original text says 'no', while the changed text implies 'some', even if minimal, because of the 'good faith' requirement. This constitutes a clear contradiction based on the 'Omissions' definition, creating inconsistency in the document.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be gover ned by the laws of the jurisdiction in which the Disclosing Party is located (or if the Disclosing Party is based in more than one country, the country in which its headquarters are located) (the "Territory") and the parties agree to submit disputes arisin g out of or in connection with this Agreement to the non -exclusive of the courts in the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by principles of fairness and equity, and the parties agree to resolve any disputes through amicable negotiation or mediation.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specification that the agreement is governed by the laws of a specific jurisdiction (the Disclosing Party's location) and replacing it with 'principles of fairness and equity' and a requirement for 'amicable negotiation or mediation,' the modified text eliminates the clear legal framework for dispute resolution. This causes contradiction because it does not specify which jurisdictions the principles of fairness and equity should be judged by. This will create uncertainty and potential unenforceability, as there's no clear legal basis for interpreting and enforcing the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by principles of fairness and equity, and the parties agree to resolve any disputes through amicable negotiation or mediation. [Receiving Party] Signature ___________________________ Name and Tile:_______________________ Address: ____________________________ Date: __________ ____________________
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the agreement is governed by the laws of the jurisdiction in which the Disclosing Party is located. The changed text replaces this with 'principles of fairness and equity' and a requirement for 'amicable negotiation or mediation.' The explanation states that this change eliminates the clear legal framework for dispute resolution, causing a contradiction. The contradicted text is the changed text itself. This is an example of Omissions – In Text Contradiction because essential language specifying the governing law has been removed, creating a conflict with the need for legal jurisdiction to enforce the agreement, as described in the explanation.
